name: String Cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Place cheese in a Teflon saucepan and add a dash of mahleb, salt, and black caraway seeds.
2. Heat on low until the cheese is melted thoroughly.
3. Remove from heat and drain excess water.
4. Pick up hot cheese in a lump, and make a hole in the center to resemble a doughnut.
5. Stretch and turn the cheese with both hands to form a large loop.
6. Double the strand of cheese, making two strands of even length. Repeat stretching and looping; the more you stretch, the stringier the cheese becomes.
7. Twist ends in opposite directions and intertwine the rope into a braid. Secure by placing one end through the loop of the other to lock it.
8. Set cheese aside to dry thoroughly. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate or freeze.
9. To serve, open the braid, cut one strand, and pull cheese apart into thin strands. Serve with pocket bread.
NOTE: Mahleb can be found at specialty food shops that sell Middle Eastern ingredients.
